servinfo.title: Contact Coil Assembly Removal and Installation

attention2.ptxt: Do not turn the rotor of the contact coil more than about two and a half turns clockwise or counterclockwise from the center position with steering wheel removed, otherwise the coil will break.

s1.ptxt: Remove steering wheel from steering column.
Remove screw (1), then detach steering column lower cover (2).
Remove screws (3), then detach steering column upper cover (4).
Release locking slider (2) by sliding it in arrow direction “1” and disconnect contact coil assembly connector (1).
Disconnect horn connector (2) and steering angle sensor connector (1).
Release tabs (2) and remove contact coil assembly (1).
Remove steering angle sensor from contact coil assembly, if necessary.
Check that vehicle’s front wheels are at straight-ahead position.
Install steering angle sensor to contact coil assembly securely.
Install contact coil assembly (1) with steering angle sensor to steering column securely.

item.ptxt: New contact coil assembly is supplied with contact coil set and held at its center position with a lock pin (2). Remove this lock pin after installing contact coil assembly to steering column.
New steering angle sensor is held at its center position with a lock pin. Remove this lock pin after installing steering angle sensor to contact coil assembly.

s1.ptxt: Connect contact coil assembly connector (1), steering angle sensor connector (2) and horn connector (3) to contact coil assembly and steering angle sensor securely.
Install steering column upper cover (4) with screws (3).
Install steering column lower cover (2) with screw (1).

attention2.ptxt: To prevent damage to each lead wire when installing covers, be careful not to pinch lead wire between covers.

s1.ptxt: Install steering wheel to steering column.
